title: ECS + LISP parser-combinator game foundation (Bun/TS page)
Thinking agents: quoting + clock + step-wise behavior
description: Give entities behavior programs stored as quoted s-expressions, resolved incrementally over simulation ticks. Decisions act on a world-state snapshot taken when thinking begins (stale beliefs), so latency creates emergent behavior. Movement stays teleport; the chase comes from timing. Target demo: Wolf & Sheep across >=3 locations. Forks resolved: in-place AST reduction; snapshot-at-thought-start beliefs; teleport movement; one reduction step per tick.

title: [A] Quote & code-as-data
description: Reader sugar '/quasiquote(backtick)/unquote(,); allow a component value to hold an SExpr; (quote x) and (eval x) builtins. Foundation for storing behavior programs on entities.

title: [B] Step interpreter (in-place AST reducer)
description: Pausable evaluator: reduce one redex per step, rewriting the AST in place. Partial thought is an inspectable/serializable SExpr. Special lazy rule for if. Prototype + unit-test in isolation before wiring to the clock. The spine of the feature.

title: [C] Clock & scheduler
description: Global tick; (tick) and (run N). Per-tick loop: perceive -> advance each entity's thought by one reduction -> apply finished actions. Beliefs snapshotted at thought-start via quasiquote. Decide within-tick resolution order (simultaneous vs sequential).

title: [E] Behavior language
description: Minimal expression language for decisions: if, equality/at? predicates, Self reference, perception queries (where, occupants, locations), and a tiny action vocabulary (move Self <dest>, stay). Keep vocabulary small.

title: [F] Wolf & Sheep example + tick UI
description: Sheep Mind: prefer Pasture, always avoid believed Wolf position. Wolf Mind: go to believed Sheep position. >=3 locations. UI: clock display + render each entity's in-progress thought tick by tick. Add as a loadable example program.
